item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea is mildly interesting, but not enough to warrant an upvote. The "Company does something that's some kind of business but don't really know how humanity works" has been done to death already, and you don't do much with the idea beyond that. I'll have to downvote

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded: Understood - edits will be vetoed as per author's request.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded: With some reflection on my part, I am afraid I will have to veto the proposed edit. Yes, the sites are numbered the same. But I did not have Conwell's Site-64 in mind. Besides, this SCP is based in New Zealand and it takes not too much sense for the base of operations to be a Pacific Ocean away.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer expired - edits not implemented as per request of author.
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Proposed Edits
- Proposed Edits

The edit proposed is to link the article to the Site-64 dossier, as the article mentions the site in its containment procedures and throughout the article.

Editing this:

A scanned copy of each recovered SCP-2752 instance is to be produced, and stored in Archival Sector 7 at Site-64.

To this:

A scanned copy of each recovered SCP-2752 instance is to be produced, and stored in Archival Sector 7 at Site-64.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be fair, the title is effectively a homophone of Junk Mail and the article was written way before I know of your existence or your entry into Wikidot.

I frankly don't see how you feel attacked over a matter like the username, and would like some elaboration on that front.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel attacked

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am proud to have contributed to society. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After some deliberation, I ultimately find your nitpick valid and have streamlined the interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A small nitpick;

Dr. Black: Do you recall the value?

PoI-2752-04: Perfectly, all 12 digits. I can write it down for you, if you want.

If the stated value was ¥960,000,000,000, it doesn't really make any sense for PoI-2752-04 to emphasize memorizing a 96 with ten zeroes behind it. To me, a "Perfectly, all __ digits." strikes me as as an unnatural phrase to use when referring to a number as above. If you're trying to hint to the reader about how large the sum of money was, it'll probably be better off just saying "it's a large number" or something to that effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm fairly certain that mail letters are still a thing nowadays. Of course, with the rise of electronic media, SCP-2752 becomes less of an issue over time but Neutralisation would imply that the root of the problem is eliminated somehow.

And in this case, the root of the problem remains. The compensation does not equate to cessation of the programme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't this have been Neutralized?

item.title: Update
item.content:encoded:
Removed SCP-2752-A and replace it with "subjects" (since they are otherwise normal people that are affected by a mild anomaly, and I use a similar system for SCP-2248). Hopefully the anomaly's object class feels more justified (since the anomaly would be solely mysterious postage letters that pop up randomly in New Zealand).

EDIT: And in line with my re-evaluation, I'm afraid I have to downgrade this to Euclid. It's not as dangerous as Keter…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OKAY THAT THURR BACK ROUNDED GREEKY VOWEL HURR

Yeah, I figured it was meant to sink the "we're not from around here!" vibe anyway, your author's notes just sparked my inner linguist pedant. :B

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair point. Edited accordingly to sort out that issue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the article but here is a small nitpick:

SCP-2752-A04: Doctor, 'wrong' is an understatement. I've received another letter… uh, up here.

He says the word wrong in quotations as though he were quoting the interviewer but the interviewer had not used that word.
Should that be different?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's actually a Latin upsilon.

Absolutely true on that front (BTW, when you type Japanese for 'Tokyo', you have to type in 'tou-kyou'). I pretty much borrowed from the English phonetic spelling, which had that the Latin upsilon on it.

Besides, if 'humanity' from that reality has no issue with eating cellulose or disposing ink in bodies or mastering matter teleportation, what's to say that their English will be exactly the same as ours?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty neat. I will say that the omega isn't actually necessary if you want to Anglicize; a u would be perfectly acceptable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now someone has to write a Keter class who's containment area is literally VY canis majoris

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an awesome article! I especially liked how the containment procedures weren't the size of VY Canis Majoris, like in other keter articles.

In other words, it wasn't hard to read. Nicely done!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely excellent. A volatile and understated Keter is a rare thing, but you've seemingly made it happen. I'm suprised at the current upvote deficit, but I know when people start looking it'll soar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this, MrWrong. I liked this in sandbox, and I like the changes here. Always glad to help make an article good.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for making me say "…the hell?" out loud.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey the draft comes to life!

+1, you son of a gun

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wonderfully weird, +1

item.title: Thanks, and Other Matters
item.content:encoded:
Allow me to express my thanks to the people who reviewed it: Mistbourne, LordStonefish, Tuomey, Moohab etc.

And to address other matters in the article:

+ show block
– hide block

The 'Toʊ' in 'Robinson Toʊ Liz' is a rough phonetic estimation of the Japanese particle と (roughly meaning 'and'). Earlier drafts have it as 'and', but I decided I want to foreshadow it.

And yes, it is a Robinson list.

The last part (with the stamps) is a reference to the hyper-inflated banana money that Imperial Japan issued to Occupied Malaya in WWII (the regime is named Malai Baru). I will note that this article is set in the 70s, where the Japanese economic miracle was still a thing and Japanese money matters.
